question: Design a `Book` class with private members for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Implement a public parameterized constructor to initialize these members and a public method `displayBookInfo()` to print all book details.

test_cases.expected:
Title: The Martian
Author: Andy Weir
ISBN: 978-0553448138

errors: The code suffers from readability and efficiency issues. Member variable names (`_t`, `_a`, `_i`) are not descriptive. The constructor passes `std::string` parameters by value and then assigns them, leading to an unnecessary copy operation for each string. The `displayBookInfo` method uses `std::endl` on every line, which flushes the output buffer unnecessarily and can be less efficient than using `\n`.
